result:
I would give every child born tomorrow the absolute certainty that their basic needs—nutritious food, clean water, safe shelter, quality health care, and a good education—will be met for life, no matter who their parents are or where they happen to be born.  

Remove that single, sprawling source of insecurity and you unclog the funnel that today wastes more human potential than anything else. With survival guaranteed, curiosity, cooperation, and creativity would skyrocket; the energy now burned on mere scarcity would pivot to solving every other problem we’ve collected—climate, disease, conflict, even the existential worry that we’re alone in the universe.  

It’s not a utopia—people would still argue, love, fail, and dream—but it would be a world where those dramas play out on a stage that isn’t on fire beneath their feet.

prompt: If you could change the world in one way, what would it be?
